impact_on_program.next: Inject top-seam ordered pairs into a set of size ≤t*p (marked incidence with small mark), or bound max fiber of pair-counts directly via SP geometry
impact_on_program.reduction: A_SP cell paid if max N_ord≤t*p or max P_multi≤t*p/17. N_ord≤16*N reduces to fiber max — circular with Q unless pairs inject.

OPEN.name: OPEN_max_N_ord_or_P_multi
OPEN.note: N_ord ≤ (pack−1)·N reduces A_SP cost to a multiple of max fiber size — circular with the Q atom unless a separate pair mark injects seam pairs into a small set.
OPEN.statement: Prove max_z N_ord(z) ≤ t·p or max_z P_multi(z) ≤ t·p/17 (or any U with pack·U ≤ remaining safe budget). CAS route: larger dyadic scans (Sage/PARI); relate max N_ord to max fiber N(z) via N_ord ≤ (pack−1)N; still needs fiber control unless a direct pair injection into [t]×F_p exists.

ambient_CS.consequence: sum_z N_ord(z) ≤ binom(n,j−e) · binom(n−j+e,e) · max(floor((n−j+e)/e)−1,0). This bounds the SUM over z, not max_z N_ord(z). Average N_ord ≤ that quantity / p^w (if every z appeared — only an envelope).
ambient_CS.name: ambient_CS_pair_bound
proof: Free-1: fixed high e−1 monic coeffs, vary constant — unique pencil per split monic of that form.
Distinct fully split CS mates are pairwise disjoint (v2/v6 free-1), so pencil size ≤ floor(n'/e).
Each of binom(n',e) sets contributes ≤ pack−1 outgoing ordered mates.
ambient_CS.statement: For any ambient Ω of size n' and e≥1, Nord_CS(e;Ω) ≤ binom(n',e) · max(floor(n'/e)−1, 0). Proof: each e-subset lies in at most one free-1 pencil; that pencil has size ≤ floor(n'/e); hence each e-set has at most max(floor(n'/e)−1,0) CS mates; double-count ordered pairs.

degree.name: topseam_degree_bound
proof: Neighbors of S=C∪U are C∪V for V in the same core pencil U(C,z), V≠U.
degree.statement: In G_z, the degree of any vertex S is at most pack_ceil(S)−1 ≤ pack_ceil−1 = 17-1 = 16, where pack_ceil = floor((n−|C_can(S)|)/e) ≤ floor((n−(j−e))/e) = floor((n−j+e)/e) and deployed pack_ceil = 17.

global_sum.name: global_sum_N_ord_equals_padded_CS_pairs
proof: Top-seam ordered pair (S,S')=(C∪U,C∪V) with U≠V CS is uniquely written as pad C and CS pair (U,V) in D\\C (v16: top-seam ⇔ CS sides).
Phi_w(C∪U)=Phi_w(C∪V) always for CS sides (deg difference =|C|=j−w−1), so the pair lies in exactly one fiber z=Phi_w(C∪U).
Summing over fibers counts each such geometric pair once.
global_sum.statement: sum_z N_ord(z) = sum_{C ⊂ D, |C|=j−e} Nord_CS(e; D\\C), where Nord_CS(e;Ω) is the number of ordered pairs of distinct e-subsets of Ω whose monic locators differ by a nonzero constant (free-1 CS pair).

handshaking.name: A_SP_handshaking_cost_chain
proof: Within a pencil of size k the directed complete graph without loops has k(k−1) arcs; sum over multi pencils is N_ord. Each vertex in a multi pencil has out-degree k−1 ≥ 1.
Hence A = #verts with deg≥1 ≤ sum deg = N_ord (actually sum deg = N_ord counts only multi pencils; singleton deg=0).
v17: A ≤ pack·P. Degree bound: N_ord = sum deg ≤ Δ·A with Δ=pack−1.

payment.name: A_SP_payment_criteria

numbers.global_sum_envelope_log2_hint: sum_z N_ord uses binom(n,core)*binom(A+e,e)*(pack-1); far above t*p — only max_z constraints help the cell.

payment.open: Prove C1 or C2 at deployed (or a weaker budget that still fits B*).
payment.statement:
Any of the following implies max |A_SP| ≤ t·p:
  (C1) max_z N_ord(z) ≤ t·p = 143763024447376
  (C2) max_z P_multi(z) ≤ floor(t·p / pack) = 8456648496904
  (C3) max_z |A_SP(z)| ≤ t·p (tautological target).
Then A_SP is a first-match cell with printed support cost ≤ t·p, matching the generated-field image budget scale (v1).

title: P_multi / |A_SP| graph cost laws and payment criteria
